title: Replace Employee ID With The Unique Identifier
key_pattern: LEFT JOIN NULL passthrough
notes: First instinct INNER JOIN, fixed after seeing missing rows

title: Product Sales Analysis I
topic: Advanced Select and Joins — INNER JOIN
key_pattern: INNER JOIN
notes: Solved cold first try

title: Customer Who Visited but Did Not Make Any Transactions
key_pattern: Anti-join LEFT JOIN + IS NULL + GROUP BY
notes: Reasoned through approach before writing, no wrong turns

title: Rising Temperature
key_pattern: Self-join on date offset
notes: Immediately recognized self-join, solved cold

title: Average Time of Process per Machine
key_pattern: Self-join with activity_type role pinning in ON clause
notes: Multiple wrong turns: ROUND cast, < vs <=, OR trick — final insight was pin roles via activity_type not timestamp ordering

title: Employee Bonus
key_pattern: Anti-join with OR filter
notes: Anti-join applied automatically, third rep of the pattern

title: Students and Examinations
key_pattern: CROSS JOIN + LEFT JOIN + COUNT(right_col)
notes: CROSS JOIN clicked fast; needed GROUP BY completeness explanation and COUNT(right_col) nudge

title: Managers with at Least 5 Direct Reports
key_pattern: Self-join + GROUP BY + HAVING
notes: Logic correct first try; GROUP BY completeness error again (second time today); good discussion on multi-column GROUP BY and HAVING vs WHERE

title: Confirmation Rate
key_pattern: LEFT JOIN + AVG(bool::integer) + COALESCE + ROUND
notes: Multiple iterations: COUNT(bool) trap, boolean cast, COALESCE for NULL, rounding — introduced COALESCE and conditional aggregation

title: Not Boring Movies
key_pattern: id % 2 for odd/even, <> vs NOT LIKE without wildcards
notes: Quick solve. Mental model for LIKE was wrong (thought it was a contains check) but both first and second attempts pass — LIKE without wildcards is exact match

title: Average Selling Price
topic: Basic Aggregate Functions — weighted average + LEFT JOIN
key_pattern: SUM(price*units)/SUM(units), BETWEEN in ON not WHERE, ::numeric cast, COALESCE
notes: Multi-step problem: wrong formula (AVG), missing GROUP BY (third time), LEFT JOIN + WHERE nullification, integer division — each fix unlocked the next problem

title: Project Employees I
topic: Advanced Select and Joins — JOIN + AVG
key_pattern: INNER JOIN + AVG(int returns numeric) + GROUP BY
notes: First clean first-try solve with GROUP BY included pre-emptively. No runtime error needed. AVG vs SUM/SUM distinction made explicit.

title: Percentage of Users Attended a Contest
key_pattern: GROUP BY contest + scalar subquery (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) as denominator
notes: First instinct was LEFT JOIN users+register — unnecessary. Key insight: unregistered users only affect denominator, which a scalar subquery handles. First introduction to scalar subquery in SELECT.

title: Queries Quality and Percentage
topic: Basic Aggregate Functions — CASE WHEN + conditional aggregation
key_pattern: CASE WHEN for conditional count, AVG(int::numeric/int) for ratio, SUM::numeric for decimal percentage
notes: First CASE WHEN introduction. COUNT(boolean) trap hit again (second time). ::numeric applied independently on SUM but needed prompting for AVG argument. GROUP BY clean third rep.

title: Monthly Transactions I
key_pattern: TO_CHAR(date, YYYY-MM) for month grouping, CASE WHEN for conditional count and sum, COUNT(*) vs COUNT(col)
notes: COUNT(boolean) trap hit for third time — still not automatic. COUNT(country) returned 0 for null country group — caught from output. CASE WHEN applied independently. GROUP BY clean fourth rep.

title: Immediate Food Delivery II
topic: Basic Aggregate Functions — derived table subquery + two-condition JOIN
key_pattern: Subquery in FROM to get MIN per group, JOIN back on customer_id + order_date, CASE WHEN for immediate check
notes: First derived-table subquery. Wrong JOIN condition first (pref_date instead of order_date). Integer division again. COUNT(boolean) probed cold at session start — got it right.

title: Game Play Analysis IV
topic: Window Functions — derived table + date offset fraction
key_pattern: Derived-table subquery + JOIN on player_id + event_date = first_login+1, scalar subquery for total distinct players
notes: Second application of derived-table pattern from LC #1174. ::numeric applied independently first time. Misread fraction as percentage initially. COUNT DISTINCT for total players.

title: Number of Unique Subjects Taught by Each Teacher
key_pattern: COUNT(DISTINCT subject_id) per group
notes: Clean first solve. Brief confusion about whether subject needed its own GROUP BY level — resolved himself.

title: User Activity for the Past 30 Days I
topic: Sorting and Grouping — date range + COUNT DISTINCT
key_pattern: COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) per day + Postgres date range: >= date::date - INTERVAL 'N days'
notes: Multiple date arithmetic issues: missing quotes, -30 vs -29, BETWEEN precedence failure, MySQL vs Postgres INTERVAL syntax.

title: Product Sales Analysis III
topic: Advanced Select and Joins — derived-table subquery
key_pattern: Derived-table subquery (3rd application): MIN(year) per product, two-condition JOIN to recover full row
notes: Got stuck on 'I have the aggregate but can't get the full row'. Connected to prior pattern after one prompt. Missing year condition in JOIN on first draft.

title: Classes More Than 5 Students
key_pattern: GROUP BY + HAVING COUNT >= N
notes: Clean first solve, no coaching needed.

title: Find Followers Count
topic: Sorting and Grouping — GROUP BY + COUNT
key_pattern: GROUP BY + COUNT + ORDER BY
notes: Forgot ORDER BY on first write.

title: Biggest Single Number
topic: Subqueries — outer MAX over filtered subquery
key_pattern: SELECT MAX(num) FROM (GROUP BY + HAVING COUNT = 1 subquery); MAX() on empty = NULL
notes: First tried everything in one GROUP BY. Then reached for unnecessary JOIN. Landed on clean outer SELECT MAX from subquery.

title: Customers Who Bought All Products
key_pattern: HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT product_key) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Product)
notes: First instinct was JOIN. Redirected to counting. Missing DISTINCT, stray comma, missing parens around subquery — all caught and fixed.
